segments.text: Susan James came back to us on the way up. Isaac Szpindel did not.

runs.t: Susan James came back to us on the way up. Isaac Szpindel did not.

segments.text: We stripped wordlessly in the decon balloon. Bates, first out of her suit, reached for Szpindel but the Gang stopped her with a hand and a headshake. Personae segued one into another as they stripped the body. Susan removed helmet and backpack and breastplate. Cruncher peeled away the silvery leaded skin from collar to toe. Sascha stripped the jumpsuit and left the pale flesh naked and exposed. Except for the gloves. They left his feedback gloves in place; their fingertips forever tactile, the flesh inside forever numb. Through it all, Szpindel stared unblinking beneath the hole in his forehead. His glazed eyes focused on distant quasars.

runs.t: We stripped wordlessly in the decon balloon. Bates, first out of her suit, reached for Szpindel but the Gang stopped her with a hand and a headshake. Personae segued one into another as they stripped the body. Susan removed helmet and backpack and breastplate. Cruncher peeled away the silvery leaded skin from collar to toe. Sascha stripped the jumpsuit and left the pale flesh naked and exposed. Except for the gloves. They left his feedback gloves in place; their fingertips forever tactile, the flesh inside forever numb. Through it all, Szpindel stared unblinking beneath the hole in his forehead. His glazed eyes focused on distant quasars.

segments.text: I expected Michelle to appear in her turn and close them, but she never did.

runs.t: I expected Michelle to appear in her turn and close them, but she never did.

segments.text: "You have eyes, but you do not see"

runs.t: "You have eyes, but you do not see"

segments.text: —Jesus the Nazorean

runs.t: —Jesus the Nazorean

segments.text: I don't know how to feel about this, I thought. He was a good man. He was decent, he was kind to me, even when he didn't know I was listening in. I didn't know him long— he wasn't a friend exactly— but still. I should miss him. I should mourn.

runs.t: I don't know how to feel about this
, I thought.
He was a good man. He was decent, he was kind to me, even when he didn't know I was listening in. I didn't know him long— he wasn't a friend exactly— but still. I should miss him. I should mourn.

segments.text: I should feel more than this sick sinking fear that I could be next...

runs.t: I should feel more than this sick sinking fear that I could be next...

segments.text: Sarasti hadn't wasted any time. Szpindel's replacement met us as we emerged, freshly thawed, nicotine-scented. The rehydration of his flesh was ongoing— saline bladders clung to each thigh—although it would never entirely erase the sharpness of his features. His bones cracked when he moved.

runs.t: Sarasti hadn't wasted any time. Szpindel's replacement met us as we emerged, freshly thawed, nicotine-scented. The rehydration of his flesh was ongoing— saline bladders clung to each thigh—although it would never entirely erase the sharpness of his features. His bones cracked when he moved.

segments.text: He looked past me and took the body. "Susan—Michelle...I—"

runs.t: He looked past me and took the body. "Susan—Michelle...I—"

segments.text: The gang turned away.

runs.t: The gang turned away.

segments.text: He coughed, began fumbling a body condom over the corpse. "Sarasti wants everyone in the drum."

runs.t: He coughed, began fumbling a body condom over the corpse. "Sarasti wants everyone in the drum."

segments.text: "We're hot," Bates said. Even cut short, the excursion had piled up a lethal Seivert count. Faint nausea tickled the back of my throat.

runs.t: "We're hot," Bates said. Even cut short, the excursion had piled up a lethal Seivert count. Faint nausea tickled the back of my throat.

segments.text: "Decontaminate later." One long pull of a zipper and Szpindel was gone, engulfed in an oily gray shroud. "You—" he turned in my direction, pointed at the scorched holes in my jumpsuit. "With me."

runs.t: "Decontaminate later." One long pull of a zipper and Szpindel was gone, engulfed in an oily gray shroud. "You—" he turned in my direction, pointed at the scorched holes in my jumpsuit. "With me."

segments.text: Robert Cunningham. Another prototype. Dark-haired, hollow-cheeked, a jaw you could use as a ruler. Both smoother and harsher than the man he had replaced. Where Szpindel had ticced and jerked as if static-charged, Cunningham's face held all the expression of a wax dummy's. The wetware that ran those muscles had been press-ganged into other pursuits. Even the tremors that afflicted the rest of his body were muted, soothed by the nicotine he drew with every second breath.

runs.t: Robert Cunningham. Another prototype. Dark-haired, hollow-cheeked, a jaw you could use as a ruler. Both smoother and harsher than the man he had replaced. Where Szpindel had ticced and jerked as if static-charged, Cunningham's face held all the expression of a wax dummy's. The wetware that ran those muscles had been press-ganged into other pursuits. Even the tremors that afflicted the rest of his body were muted, soothed by the nicotine he drew with every second breath.

segments.text: He held no cigarette now. He held only the shrouded body of his hard-luck primary and his ongoing, freshly thawed distaste for the ship's synthesist. His fingers trembled.

runs.t: He held no cigarette now. He held only the shrouded body of his hard-luck primary and his ongoing, freshly thawed distaste for the ship's synthesist. His fingers trembled.

segments.text: Bates and the Gang moved silently up the spine. Cunningham and I followed, guiding the Shroud of Szpindel between us. My leg and side were stinging again, now that Cunningham had reminded them to. There wouldn't be much he could do about them, though. The beams would have cauterized the flesh on their way through, and if they'd hit anything vital I'd have been dead already.

runs.t: Bates and the Gang moved silently up the spine. Cunningham and I followed, guiding the Shroud of Szpindel between us. My leg and side were stinging again, now that Cunningham had reminded them to. There wouldn't be much he could do about them, though. The beams would have cauterized the flesh on their way through, and if they'd hit anything vital I'd have been dead already.

segments.text: At the hatch we broke into single-file: Szpindel first, Cunningham pushing at his heels. By the time I emerged into the drum Bates and the gang were already down on deck and taking their usual seats. Sarasti, in the flesh, watched them from the end of the conference table.

runs.t: At the hatch we broke into single-file: Szpindel first, Cunningham pushing at his heels. By the time I emerged into the drum Bates and the gang were already down on deck and taking their usual seats. Sarasti, in the flesh, watched them from the end of the conference table.

segments.text: His eyes were naked. From this angle the soft, full-spectrum light of the drum washed the shine from them. If you didn't look too closely, for too long, you might almost think those eyes were Human.

runs.t: His eyes were naked. From this angle the soft, full-spectrum light of the drum washed the shine from them. If you didn't look too closely, for too long, you might almost think those eyes were Human.

segments.text: BioMed had been spun down for my arrival. Cunningham pointed to a diagnostic couch on a section of the stilled deck that served as our infirmary; I floated over and strapped myself in. Two meters away, past a waist-high guard rail that had risen from the deck, the rest of the drum rolled smoothly past. It slung Bates and the Gang and Sarasti around like weights on a string.

runs.t: BioMed had been spun down for my arrival. Cunningham pointed to a diagnostic couch on a section of the stilled deck that served as our infirmary; I floated over and strapped myself in. Two meters away, past a waist-high guard rail that had risen from the deck, the rest of the drum rolled smoothly past. It slung Bates and the Gang and Sarasti around like weights on a string.

segments.text: I tapped ConSensus to hear them. James was speaking, quietly and without expression. "I noticed a new pattern in the form-constants. Something in the grating. It looked like a signal. It got stronger as I went down the tunnel, I followed it, I blacked out. I don't remember anything more until we were on our way back. Michelle filled me in, as much as she could. That's all I know. I'm sorry."

runs.t: I tapped ConSensus to hear them. James was speaking, quietly and without expression. "I noticed a new pattern in the form-constants. Something in the grating. It looked like a signal. It got stronger as I went down the tunnel, I followed it, I blacked out. I don't remember anything more until we were on our way back. Michelle filled me in, as much as she could. That's all I know. I'm sorry."

segments.text: A hundred degrees away in the no-gee zone, Cunningham maneuvered his predecessor into a coffin with different options than those up front. I wondered if it would embark on an autopsy during the debriefing. I wondered if we'd be able to hear the sounds it made.

runs.t: A hundred degrees away in the no-gee zone, Cunningham maneuvered his predecessor into a coffin with different options than those up front. I wondered if it would embark on an autopsy during the debriefing. I wondered if we'd be able to hear the sounds it made.

segments.text: "Sascha," Sarasti said.

runs.t: "Sascha," Sarasti said.

segments.text: "Yeah." Sascha's trademark drawl infected the voice. "I was riding Mom. Went deaf dumb and stark fucking blind when she passed out. I tried to take over but something was blocking me. Michelle, I guess. Never thought she had it in her. I couldn't even see."

runs.t: "Yeah." Sascha's trademark drawl infected the voice. "I was riding Mom. Went deaf dumb and stark fucking blind when she passed out. I tried to take over but something was blocking me. Michelle, I guess. Never thought she had it in her. I couldn't even
see

segments.text: "But you don't lose consciousness."

runs.t: "But you don't lose consciousness."

segments.text: "I was awake the whole time, far as I know. Just completely in the dark."

runs.t: "I was awake the whole time, far as I know. Just completely in the dark."

segments.text: "Smell? Tactile?"

runs.t: "Smell? Tactile?"

segments.text: "I could feel it when Michelle pissed in the suit. But I didn't notice anything else."

runs.t: "I could feel it when Michelle pissed in the suit. But I didn't notice anything else."

segments.text: Cunningham was back at my side. The inevitable cigarette had appeared between his lips.

runs.t: Cunningham was back at my side. The inevitable cigarette had appeared between his lips.

segments.text: "Nothing touches you," the vampire surmised. "Nothing grabs your leg."

runs.t: "Nothing touches you," the vampire surmised. "Nothing grabs your leg."

segments.text: "No," Sascha said. She didn't believe Michelle's stories about invisible monsters. None of us did; why bother, when dementia could so easily explain anything we experienced?

runs.t: "No," Sascha said. She didn't believe Michelle's stories about invisible monsters. None of us did; why bother, when dementia could so easily explain anything we experienced?

segments.text: "Cruncher."

runs.t: "Cruncher."

segments.text: "Don't know anything," I still wasn't used to the maleness of the voice now emanating from James's throat. Cruncher was a workaholic. He hardly ever surfaced in mixed company.

runs.t: "Don't know anything," I still wasn't used to the maleness of the voice now emanating from James's throat. Cruncher was a workaholic. He hardly ever surfaced in mixed company.

segments.text: "You're there," Sarasti reminded him. "You must remember some—"

runs.t: "You're there," Sarasti reminded him. "You must remember some—"

segments.text: "Mom sent me patterns to parse. I was working on them. I'm still working on them," he added pointedly. "I didn't notice anything. Is that all?"

runs.t: "Mom sent me patterns to parse. I was working on them. I'm
still
working on them," he added pointedly. "I didn't notice anything. Is that all?"

segments.text: I'd never been able to get a good read on him. Sometimes Cruncher seemed to have more in common with the dozens of nonconscious modules working in James's head than with sentient hubs comprising the rest of the Gang. "You feel nothing?" Sarasti pressed.

runs.t: I'd never been able to get a good read on him. Sometimes Cruncher seemed to have more in common with the dozens of nonconscious modules working in James's head than with sentient hubs comprising the rest of the Gang. "You feel nothing?" Sarasti pressed.

segments.text: "Just the patterns."

runs.t: "Just the patterns."

segments.text: "Anything significant?"

runs.t: "Anything significant?"

segments.text: "Standard phenomath spirals and gratings. But I haven't finished. Can I go now?"

runs.t: "Standard phenomath spirals and gratings. But I haven't finished. Can I go now?"

segments.text: "Yes. Call Michelle, please."

runs.t: "Yes. Call Michelle, please."

segments.text: Cunningham stabbed at my wounds with anabolisers, muttering to himself. Faint blue smoke curled between us. "Isaac found some tumors," he observed.

runs.t: Cunningham stabbed at my wounds with anabolisers, muttering to himself. Faint blue smoke curled between us. "Isaac found some tumors," he observed.

segments.text: I nodded and coughed. My throat was sore. The nausea had grown heavy enough to sink below my diaphragm.

runs.t: I nodded and coughed. My throat was sore. The nausea had grown heavy enough to sink below my diaphragm.

segments.text: "Michelle." Sarasti repeated.

runs.t: "Michelle." Sarasti repeated.

segments.text: "I see some more here," Cunningham continued. "Along the bottom of your brain pan. Only a few dozen cells so far, they're not worth burning yet."

runs.t: "I see some more here," Cunningham continued. "Along the bottom of your brain pan. Only a few dozen cells so far, they're not worth burning yet."

segments.text: "Here." Michelle's voice was barely audible, even through ConSensus, but at least it was the voice of an adult. "I'm here."

runs.t: "Here." Michelle's voice was barely audible, even through ConSensus, but at least it was the voice of an adult. "I'm here."

segments.text: "What do you remember, please?"

runs.t: "What do you remember, please?"

segments.text: "I—I felt—I was just riding Mom, and then she was gone and there was no one else, so I had to—take over…"

runs.t: "I—I felt—I was just riding Mom, and then she was gone and there was no one else, so I had to—take over…"

segments.text: "Do you see the septum close?"

runs.t: "Do you see the septum close?"

segments.text: "Not really. I felt it going dark, but when I turned around we were already trapped. And then I felt something behind me, it wasn't loud or harsh it just sort of bumped, and it grabbed me, and—and—

runs.t: "Not really. I felt it going dark, but when I turned around we were already trapped. And then I felt something behind me, it wasn't loud or harsh it just sort of
bumped
, and it grabbed me, and—and—

segments.text: "I'm sorry," she said after a moment. "I'm a bit—woozy..."

runs.t: "I'm sorry," she said after a moment. "I'm a bit—woozy..."

segments.text: Sarasti waited.

runs.t: Sarasti waited.

segments.text: "Isaac," Michelle whispered. "He..."

runs.t: "Isaac," Michelle whispered. "He..."

segments.text: "Yes." A pause. "We're very sorry about that."

runs.t: "Yes." A pause. "We're very sorry about that."

segments.text: "Maybe—can he be fixed?"

runs.t: "Maybe—can he be fixed?"

segments.text: "No. There's brain damage." There was something like sympathy in the vampire's voice, the practiced affectation of an accomplished mimic. There was something else, too, an all-but-imperceptible hunger, a subtle edge of temptation. I don't think anyone heard it but me.

runs.t: "No. There's brain damage." There was something like sympathy in the vampire's voice, the practiced affectation of an accomplished mimic. There was something else, too, an all-but-imperceptible hunger, a subtle edge of
temptation
. I don't think anyone heard it but me.

segments.text: We were sick, and getting sicker. Predators are drawn to the weak and injured.

runs.t: We were sick, and getting sicker. Predators are drawn to the weak and injured.

segments.text: Michelle had fallen silent again. When she continued, her voice only faltered a little: "I can't tell you much. It grabbed me. It let me go. I went to pieces, and I can't explain why except that fucking place just does things to you, and I was—weak. I'm sorry. There's not much else to tell you."

runs.t: Michelle had fallen silent again. When she continued, her voice only faltered a little: "I can't tell you much. It grabbed me. It let me go. I went to pieces, and I can't explain why except that fucking place just
does
things to you, and I was—weak. I'm sorry. There's not much else to tell you."

segments.text: "Thank you," Sarasti said after a long moment.

runs.t: "Thank you," Sarasti said after a long moment.

segments.text: "Can I—I'd like to leave if that's okay."

runs.t: "Can I—I'd like to leave if that's okay."

segments.text: "Yes," Sarasti said. Michelle sank below the surface as the Commons rotated past. I didn't see who took her place.

runs.t: "Yes," Sarasti said. Michelle sank below the surface as the Commons rotated past. I didn't see who took her place.

segments.text: "The grunts didn't see anything," Bates remarked. "By the time we broke through the septum the tunnel behind was empty."

runs.t: "The grunts didn't see anything," Bates remarked. "By the time we broke through the septum the tunnel behind was empty."

segments.text: "Any bogey would have had plenty of time to hightail," Cunningham said. He planted his feet on the deck and grabbed a handhold; the subdrum began to move. I drifted obliquely against my restraints.

runs.t: "Any bogey would have had plenty of time to hightail," Cunningham said. He planted his feet on the deck and grabbed a handhold; the subdrum began to move. I drifted obliquely against my restraints.

segments.text: "I don't disagree," Bates said, "But if there's anything we've learned about that place, it's that we can't trust our senses."

runs.t: "I don't disagree," Bates said, "But if there's anything we've learned about that place, it's that we can't trust our senses."

segments.text: "Trust Michelle's," Sarasti said. He opened a window as I grew heavier: a grunt's-eye view of a fuzzy, bright blob weaving behind the translucent waxed-paper fibers of the skinned septum. James's headlight, from the wrong side of the barrier. The image wobbled a bit as the drone staggered through some local pocket of magnetism, then replayed. Wobbled, replayed. A six-second loop.

runs.t: "Trust Michelle's," Sarasti said. He opened a window as I grew heavier: a grunt's-eye view of a fuzzy, bright blob weaving behind the translucent waxed-paper fibers of the skinned septum. James's headlight, from the wrong side of the barrier. The image wobbled a bit as the drone staggered through some local pocket of magnetism, then replayed. Wobbled, replayed. A six-second loop.

segments.text: "See something next to the Gang."

runs.t: "See something next to the Gang."

segments.text: Non-vampires saw no such thing. Sarasti froze the image, evidently realizing as much. "Diffraction patterns aren't consistent with a single light source in open space. I see dimmer elements, reflective elements. Two dark objects close together, similar size, scattering light here—" a cursor appeared at two utterly nondescript points on the image— "and here. One's the Gang. The other's unaccounted for."

runs.t: Non-vampires saw no such thing. Sarasti froze the image, evidently realizing as much. "Diffraction patterns aren't consistent with a single light source in open space. I see dimmer elements, reflective elements. Two dark objects close together, similar size, scattering light here—" a cursor appeared at two utterly nondescript points on the image— "and here. One's the Gang. The other's unaccounted for."

segments.text: "Just a minute," Cunningham said. "If you can see it through all that, why didn't Su—why didn't Michelle see anything?"

runs.t: "Just a minute," Cunningham said. "If
you
can see it through all that, why didn't Su—why didn't
Michelle
see anything?"

segments.text: "Synesthesiac," Sarasti reminded him. "You see. She feels."

runs.t: "Synesthesiac," Sarasti reminded him. "You see. She
feels

segments.text: BioMed jerked slightly, locking into spin-synch with the drum; the guard rail sank back into the deck. Off in some far-off corner, something without eyes watched me watching it.

runs.t: BioMed jerked slightly, locking into spin-synch with the drum; the guard rail sank back into the deck. Off in some far-off corner, something without eyes watched me watching it.

segments.text: "Shit," Bates whispered. "There's someone home."

runs.t: "Shit," Bates whispered. "There's someone home."
